headline: Belgium's Long-Ball Goal Sinks a Determined Canada
byline: By Weiyi Cai, Chloé Desaulles, Alexandre Devaux, Or Fleisher, Malika Khurana, Eleanor Lutz, Allison McCann, Mark McKeague, Scott Reinhard and Bedel Saget — Nov. 23, 2022

beats.text: AL RAYYAN, Qatar — Belgium, the second-ranked team in the world and a tournament favorite, eked out a win over Canada in its opening match of the 2022 World Cup. The lone goal came at the end of the first half and started with a long ball from deep in Belgium's own end.
It started from Belgium's back line. Toby Alderweireld had possession, but there were no midfield options — Canada's attackers had dropped in to press.
Alderweireld looked up, lobbed a perfectly weighted ball over the top and bypassed the entire Canadian team.
Batshuayi held his run, careful to stay onside, moving down the center of the field to meet the ball in flight.
Canada's defenders raced to recover, but Batshuayi was one step ahead.
Batshuayi anticipated the flight of the ball, letting it bounce once before striking a half-volley into the net.
Belgium earned 3 points and now stands atop Group F, after Croatia and Morocco played to a scoreless draw on Wednesday.

methodology: The 3-D scene was created using photographs and videos of the venue for reference. The positions of the players were determined from a photograph taken at that moment during the play. The player models were created using a machine-learning model and refined manually using photographs as reference.
